steps.description: Audit and stabilize the existing backend implementation for uploads/comments/backlinks against the requested contract before adding more code, because these target files already exist but currently diverge from the spec and previously caused a large typecheck regression. Read the route implementations, app registration, env/storage helpers, auth request shape, and current OpenAPI schema files; then correct API shapes, imports, middleware usage, and route mounting approach without changing shared hub exports directly.

Implement the database foundation needed by the requested systems if it is not already fully present: verify schema/migration coverage for files, comments, and document_links tables and add or fix the numbered migration to ensure required columns, constraints, statuses, timestamps, and indexes exist for upload metadata, threaded comments, and backlinks persistence.

Finish the file upload backend in routes/files.ts to exactly match the request: POST /api/files/upload-request with Zod validation for filename/mimeType/sizeBytes, blocked dangerous extensions, UUID-safe IDs, 1GB max, files row creation with status=pending, S3 presigned PUT URL generation with 15-minute expiration when S3_UPLOADS_BUCKET is configured, local dev POST /api/files/upload/:id multipart handling that writes into uploads/, row status transition to uploaded, and GET /api/files/:id returning metadata plus download URL. Also ensure local uploads are served statically from uploads/ and that route responses align with frontend needs.

Finish the comments backend in routes/comments.ts so it uses the repository’s actual Express/auth/database conventions and matches the requested API: GET /api/documents/:id/comments grouped by comment_id, POST /api/documents/:id/comments for new threads or replies via parent_id while allowing client-provided TipTap comment_id, PATCH /api/comments/:id to edit content, POST /api/comments/:id/resolve to set resolved_at, and DELETE /api/comments/:id restricted to author or admin/owner. Make response shapes consistent with the frontend hook and comment display components.

Finish the backlinks backend in routes/backlinks.ts to match the requested endpoints and payloads: GET /api/documents/:id/backlinks returning source documents with title and type, and POST /api/documents/:id/links performing upsert/sync of document_links using extracted mentions/links from content payloads. Preserve visibility/auth behavior and validate UUIDs on all document IDs.

Update backend tests for the new and corrected APIs so the implementation is locked to the requested behavior and does not reintroduce the prior mid-step typecheck/test drift. Cover upload-request validation, blocked extensions, local upload flow, file metadata retrieval, comment thread lifecycle, resolve timestamp behavior, delete permissions, and backlinks link synchronization.

Refactor the frontend upload service to match the finalized backend contract and avoid the current mismatches that likely contributed to the prior typecheck failure. Ensure requestUpload calls /api/files/upload-request, uploadFile handles either presigned S3 PUT or local multipart POST upload, trackUploadProgress has a usable typed callback flow, and returned metadata/URLs are compatible with editor integrations.

Revise the upload tracking and navigation warning stack so it actually warns during active uploads without breaking navigation semantics. Keep the existing tracker utility but align it with the upload service/editor usage, and fix the UploadContext plus UploadNavigationWarning component so proceed/stay behavior is meaningful and type-safe.

Update editor upload integrations to use the corrected upload service/tracker contract and ensure uploaded image/file URLs resolve correctly in TipTap. This includes image paste/drop/file-picker behavior and non-image attachment handling, keeping navigation warning hooks intact while avoiding stale editor updates or incompatible response parsing.

Refactor the comments TanStack Query hook to match the corrected backend payloads and mutation endpoints, including list, create thread, reply, edit, resolve, and delete operations. Remove current response-shape mismatches so downstream editor/comment UI code typechecks cleanly.

Align the inline comment rendering/editor integration with the finalized comment thread shape from useCommentsQuery so inline threads, replies, and resolved_at display continue to work. Fix any field-name mismatches between comment_id/comments arrays and the decoration logic.
Update frontend tests that cover upload and comment editor behavior so the new service and hook contracts are exercised and the previous regression does not recur through stale expectations.

messages.content:
Implement file upload and inline comments systems for Ship.

For file uploads, create `routes/files.ts` with auth middleware: POST /api/files/upload-request (validates filename/mimeType/sizeBytes with Zod, blocks dangerous extensions like .exe/.bat/.sh/.dll/.jar/.ps1, creates a files row with status=pending, returns a presigned S3 upload URL if S3_UPLOADS_BUCKET is configured or a local upload endpoint for dev), POST /api/files/upload/:id (for local dev: accepts multipart form data, writes to uploads/ directory, updates files row to status=uploaded), GET /api/files/:id (returns file metadata + download URL). For S3: use @aws-sdk/client-s3 and @aws-sdk/s3-request-presigner with 15-minute URL expiration. For local dev: serve uploads/ as static files. Set max file size to 1GB. Validate UUID format on all ID params to prevent path traversal.

For comments, create `routes/comments.ts` with: GET /api/documents/:id/comments (list all comment threads for a document, grouped by comment_id), POST /api/documents/:id/comments (create a new comment thread with comment_id matching the TipTap comment mark, or reply to existing via parent_id), PATCH /api/comments/:id (update comment content), POST /api/comments/:id/resolve (set resolved_at timestamp), DELETE /api/comments/:id (only author or admin can delete). Create `routes/backlinks.ts` with: GET /api/documents/:id/backlinks (query document_links where target_id matches, return source documents with title and type), POST /api/documents/:id/links (upsert document_links from extracted mentions/links in content).

On the frontend, create `src/services/upload.ts` with: requestUpload (calls upload-request endpoint), uploadFile (either uploads directly to presigned S3 URL or to the local endpoint), trackUploadProgress. Create `src/services/uploadTracker.ts` to track active uploads and warn on navigation (UploadNavigationWarning component). Create `src/hooks/useCommentsQuery.ts` for fetching and mutating comments via TanStack Query.

Verify: upload an image via the API, confirm it returns a valid URL, embed it in a TipTap document via the editor, confirm it renders. Create a comment thread on a document, reply to it, resolve it, confirm the resolved_at timestamp is set.

- I did not add stronger constraints to `comments` beyond indexing, since the requested route behavior will be enforced in the API layer and the current schema already supports threaded comments with `comment_id`, `parent_id`, `resolved_at`, `author_id`, and `workspace_id`.
